root_text.an11.13:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.
root_text.an11.13:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ vassāvāsaṁ upagantukāmo hoti.
root_text.an11.13:2.5: Tattha kammantañceva adhiṭṭhahissāmi, bhagavantañca lacchāmi kālena kālaṁ dassanāyā”ti.
root_text.an11.13:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ vassāvāsaṁ upagacchi.
root_text.an11.13:3.2: Nandiyopi kho sakko sāvatthiyaṁ vassāvāsaṁ upagacchi.
root_text.an11.13:3.3: Tattha kammantañceva adhiṭṭhāsi, bhagavantañca labhi kālena kālaṁ dassanāya.
root_text.an11.13:3.4: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū bhagavato cīvarakammaṁ karonti:
root_text.an11.13:4.4: Atha kho nandiyo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho nandiyo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an11.13:4.8: Tesaṁ no, bhante, nānāvihārehi viharataṁ kenassa vihārena vihātabban”ti?
root_text.an11.13:5.2: Etaṁ kho, nandiya, tumhākaṁ patirūpaṁ kulaputtānaṁ, yaṁ tumhe tathāgataṁ upasaṅkamitvā puccheyyātha:
root_text.an11.13:5.3: ‘tesaṁ no, bhante, nānāvihārehi viharataṁ kenassa vihārena vihātabban’ti?
root_text.an11.13:5.4: Saddho kho, nandiya, ārādhako hoti, no assaddho;
root_text.an11.13:5.10: Imesu kho te, nandiya, chasu dhammesu patiṭṭhāya pañcasu dhammesu ajjhattaṁ sati upaṭṭhāpetabbā.
root_text.an11.13:6.2: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi, satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.an11.13:6.3: Iti kho te, nandiya, tathāgataṁ ārabbha ajjhattaṁ sati upaṭṭhāpetabbā.
root_text.an11.13:7.1: Puna caparaṁ tvaṁ, nandiya, dhammaṁ anussareyyāsi:
root_text.an11.13:7.2: ‘svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo sandiṭṭhiko akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī’ti.
root_text.an11.13:7.3: Iti kho te, nandiya, dhammaṁ ārabbha ajjhattaṁ sati upaṭṭhāpetabbā.
root_text.an11.13:8.1: Puna caparaṁ tvaṁ, nandiya, kalyāṇamitte anussareyyāsi:
root_text.an11.13:8.3: yassa me kalyāṇamittā anukampakā atthakāmā ovādakā anusāsakā’ti.
root_text.an11.13:8.4: Iti kho te, nandiya, kalyāṇamitte ārabbha ajjhattaṁ sati upaṭṭhāpetabbā.
root_text.an11.13:9.1: Puna caparaṁ tvaṁ, nandiya, attano cāgaṁ anussareyyāsi:
root_text.an11.13:9.3: yohaṁ maccheramalapariyuṭṭhitāya pajāya vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasāmi muttacāgo payatapāṇi vossaggarato yācayogo dānasaṁvibhāgarato’ti.
root_text.an11.13:9.4: Iti kho te, nandiya, cāgaṁ ārabbha ajjhattaṁ sati upaṭṭhāpetabbā.
root_text.an11.13:10.1: Puna caparaṁ tvaṁ, nandiya, devatā anussareyyāsi:
root_text.an11.13:10.2: ‘yā devatā atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devatānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapannā, tā karaṇīyaṁ attano na samanupassanti katassa vā paticayaṁ’.
root_text.an11.13:10.3: Seyyathāpi, nandiya, bhikkhu asamayavimutto karaṇīyaṁ attano na samanupassati katassa vā paticayaṁ;
root_text.an11.13:10.4: evamevaṁ kho, nandiya, yā tā devatā atikkammeva kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ devatānaṁ sahabyataṁ aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapannā, tā karaṇīyaṁ attano na samanupassanti katassa vā paticayaṁ.
root_text.an11.13:10.5: Iti kho te, nandiya, devatā ārabbha ajjhattaṁ sati upaṭṭhāpetabbā.
root_text.an11.13:11.1: Imehi kho, nandiya, ekādasahi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako pajahateva pāpake akusale dhamme, na upādiyati.
root_text.an11.13:11.2: Seyyathāpi, nandiya, kumbho nikkujjo vamateva udakaṁ, no vantaṁ paccāvamati;
root_text.an11.13:11.3: seyyathāpi vā pana, nandiya, sukkhe tiṇadāye aggi mutto ḍahaññeva gacchati, no daḍḍhaṁ paccudāvattati;
root_text.an11.13:11.4: evamevaṁ kho, nandiya, imehi ekādasahi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako pajahateva pāpake akusale dhamme, na upādiyatī”ti.

translation_text.an11.13:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near Kapilavatthu in the Banyan Tree Monastery.
translation_text.an11.13:1.2: Now at that time the Buddha wanted to commence the rains residence at Sāvatthī.
translation_text.an11.13:2.1: Nandiya the Sakyan heard about this,
translation_text.an11.13:2.4: “Why don’t I also commence the rains residence at Sāvatthī.
translation_text.an11.13:2.5: There I can focus on my work and from time to time get to see the Buddha.”
translation_text.an11.13:3.1: So the Buddha commenced the rains residence in Sāvatthī,
translation_text.an11.13:3.3: There he focused on his work and from time to time got to see the Buddha.
translation_text.an11.13:3.4: At that time several mendicants were making a robe for the Buddha, thinking that
translation_text.an11.13:3.5: when his robe was finished and the three months of the rains residence had passed the Buddha would set out wandering.
translation_text.an11.13:4.1: Nandiya the Sakyan heard about this.
translation_text.an11.13:4.4: He went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an11.13:4.6: several mendicants are making a robe for the Buddha, thinking that
translation_text.an11.13:4.7: when his robe was finished and the three months of the rains residence had passed the Buddha would set out wandering.
translation_text.an11.13:4.8: Now, we spend our life in various ways. Which of these should we practice?”
translation_text.an11.13:5.2: It’s appropriate that gentlemen such as you come to me and ask:
translation_text.an11.13:5.3: ‘We spend our life in various ways. Which of these should we practice?’
translation_text.an11.13:5.4: The faithful succeed, not the faithless.
translation_text.an11.13:5.5: The ethical succeed, not the unethical.
translation_text.an11.13:5.6: The energetic succeed, not the lazy.
translation_text.an11.13:5.7: The mindful succeed, not the unmindful.
translation_text.an11.13:5.8: Those with immersion succeed, not those without immersion.
translation_text.an11.13:5.9: The wise succeed, not the witless.
translation_text.an11.13:5.10: When you’re grounded on these six things, go on to establish mindfulness on five further things internally.
translation_text.an11.13:6.1: Firstly, you should recollect the Realized One:
translation_text.an11.13:6.2: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.an11.13:6.3: In this way you should establish mindfulness internally based on the Realized One.
translation_text.an11.13:7.1: Furthermore, you should recollect the teaching:
translation_text.an11.13:7.2: ‘The teaching is well explained by the Buddha—apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.’
translation_text.an11.13:7.3: In this way you should establish mindfulness internally based on the teaching.
translation_text.an11.13:8.1: Furthermore, you should recollect your good friends:
translation_text.an11.13:8.3: to have good friends who advise and instruct me out of kindness and sympathy.’
translation_text.an11.13:8.4: In this way you should establish mindfulness internally based on good friends.
translation_text.an11.13:9.1: Furthermore, you should recollect your own generosity:
translation_text.an11.13:9.3: Among people with hearts full of the stain of stinginess I live at home rid of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, loving to let go, committed to charity, loving to give and to share.’
translation_text.an11.13:9.4: In this way you should establish mindfulness internally based on generosity.
translation_text.an11.13:10.1: Furthermore, you should recollect the deities:
translation_text.an11.13:10.2: ‘There are deities who, surpassing the company of deities that consume edible food, are reborn in a certain host of mind-made deities. They don’t see in themselves anything more to do, or anything that needs improvement.’
translation_text.an11.13:10.3: An irreversibly freed mendicant doesn’t see in themselves anything more to do, or anything that needs improvement.
translation_text.an11.13:10.4: In the same way, Nandiya, there are deities who, surpassing the company of deities that consume edible food, are reborn in a certain host of mind-made deities. They don’t see in themselves anything more to do, or anything that needs improvement.
translation_text.an11.13:10.5: In this way you should establish mindfulness internally based on the deities.
translation_text.an11.13:11.1: A noble disciple who possesses these eleven qualities gives up bad, unskillful qualities and doesn’t cling to them.
translation_text.an11.13:11.2: It’s like when a pot full of water is tipped over, so the water drains out and doesn’t go back in.
translation_text.an11.13:11.3: Suppose there was an uncontrolled fire. It advances burning up dry woodlands and doesn’t turn back over what it has burned.
translation_text.an11.13:11.4: In the same way, a noble disciple who possesses these eleven qualities gives up bad, unskillful qualities and doesn’t cling to them.”

variant_text.an11.13:3.3: adhiṭṭhāsi → adhiṭṭhāya (sya-all); adhiṭṭhāti (mr) | labhi → lacchati (sya-all, mr)
variant_text.an11.13:10.2: kabaḷīkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ → kabaliṅkārāhārabhakkhānaṁ (bj); kabaliṅkārabhakkhānaṁ (si); kabaḷīkārabhakkhānaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an11.13:11.2: nikkujjo → nikujjo (mr) | paccāvamati → pacchā vamati (bj); paccāmasati (sya-all)
